cityDNA.coreFantasy: Tormund Vale is a giant river-valley city where canal works, grain halls, civic relief, healing institutions, and training arenas turn floodplain scale into public responsibility.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Tormund Vale should feel immediately distinct: giants civic life is organized around Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, and Arena / Training, with Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud as the citywide symbol.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The city turns Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas, Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin, and Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory into a working urban system rather than surface decoration.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Use Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, visible Lock House Beacon, the central Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and the skyline-scale Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud as the core visual grammar.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A giants population whose institutions make daily life legible through section anchors: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city is symbolized by Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud, Lock House Beacon, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and the repeating civic pattern of hall, landmark, monument, and nook in every section.

canonLocks: Tormund Vale is built for its giants population and should not be genericized into a neutral fantasy city.
The Wonder is Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud.
The Beacon is Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry.
The five section spines are Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training.
The tactical board uses 25 district slots, with District 13 as the seat-of-power center.
Exact sidecar names for civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks must be preserved verbatim.
provisionalCanon: Citizens likely treat Lock House Beacon as the practical public signal point for citywide alerts and Conjunction response.
Daily politics likely moves through the named section institutions rather than through faction headquarters.
The city’s civic magic expresses through Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory when visible in public spaces.
rejectedReads: Tormund Vale as a generic fantasy settlement with the culture applied as ornament only.
Replacing exact section anchors with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, archive, or court when the sidecar provides a proper name.
Treating the Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power as interchangeable skyline props instead of civic machinery.
cautionFlags: Do not add unsupported per-district landmark names beyond the source-backed section anchors.
Keep all 25 districts, but derive their specific identity from the five section spines unless new district-specific sidecars exist.
Preserve the material language: Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas.
Preserve the terrain/water logic: Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin; water context: River.

environment.environmentalLogic: Environment should make the city’s institutions readable: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training must feel embedded in Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin, with water handled as River and public magic appearing as Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory.

skyline: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud dominates the far skyline and should be larger than every section landmark.
Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry anchors the central District 13 civic mass.
Lock House Beacon is the section-1 signal landmark and must be readable from nearby districts.
Section skyline rhythm: Lock House Beacon, Grain Hall, Relief Hall, Healing Hall, Training Arena.

civilization.people: The population is giants. Civic scenes should show them using the city’s architecture naturally: gathering near Lock House Beacon, moving through Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, and treating the named anchors as practical institutions.
socialClasses: seat-of-power officials attached to Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
section hall administrators and permit keepers
craft, market, transport, religious, or military guild households depending on district spine
street vendors, carriers, maintenance workers, and household service families
visitors and petitioners who navigate the city through public anchors
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry and expressed locally through the five section civic halls. Authority should be visible in courts, archives, public signals, permits, and named monuments rather than hidden abstraction.
civilization.religion: Religious and symbolic life should draw on Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory, the Wonder’s public symbolism, and whichever section anchors carry sacred or ceremonial roles.
customs: citizens use named halls and nooks as practical meeting points
public signals from the Beacon interrupt ordinary commerce and movement
section monuments preserve civic memory and local identity
visitors orient themselves by Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section major landmarks
formal petitions and judgments move toward District 13
bodyLanguage: citizens make room for official messengers near beacon approaches
locals gesture by landmark rather than by street name
public posture changes around courts, monuments, and halls
market or service workers keep one eye on signal points during tense periods
speechPatterns: street speech abbreviates section landmarks but not Wonder, Beacon, or Seat names
public announcements use practical civic language rather than poetic vagueness

streets.appearance: Street views should show Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, pedestrians using section halls, and sightlines back to Lock House Beacon or Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry where appropriate.
streets.movement: Movement is organized by section spine: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training. Districts feed toward their civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook before converging on District 13 for authority scenes.
streets.lighting: Use civic blue illumination for official signal moments, practical lantern light for night scenes, and material-specific highlights on Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce terrain and water logic: Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin; water context River.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings emphasize deliveries, hall openings, street maintenance, market preparation, and official notices; afternoons build toward hearings, training, guild work, or section-specific labor.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings compress around lanterns, food stalls, guarded thresholds, quieter civic errands, and beacon visibility against darker streets.

prestigious: Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry document ateliers
Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud ceremonial suppliers

housing.wealthy: Residences near Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry or high-status section courts use the best Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, private terraces, and controlled views of civic landmarks.
housing.middle: Households cluster above shops and along civic streets, sharing courtyards, balconies, workrooms, and access to section halls.
housing.poor: Lower-status housing is practical, crowded, and close to service yards or labor streets, but still uses local motifs and public landmarks for orientation.

wonder.name: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of giants identity made public and monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A one-per-city wonder: spectacular, unmistakable, and larger than every section landmark. It turns Tormund Vale's geography, race identity, material culture, and civic magic into one public symbol. Built for Tormund Vale's giants population, it uses Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas across Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin; water context: River; magic expression: Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Gi
wonder.civicRole: A unifying civic symbol larger than section landmarks; it gives Tormund Vale its skyline identity and public myth.
storyHooks: a public ceremony at the Wonder changes city mood
maintenance access reveals hidden civic routes
the Wonder becomes the reference point during a beacon state

beacon.name: Lock House Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: The public signal point where citywide warning becomes local action.
beacon.visualRole: A largest local landmark in this section, subordinate only to the city wonder and citadel. It belongs to Section 1 (Canal / Riverworks) of Tormund Vale and should use White Limestone Civic Blocks, Reed Pavilions, Coral Terraces, timber and canvas materials, Hanging Gardens, Canal Reflections, Lantern Lit, and the giants visual language. Design focus: wet stone, lock houses, sluice gates, waterwheel yards, towpaths. T
beacon.civicRole: The Section 1 signal landmark; citizens watch it for alerts, public notices, and Conjunction response.
storyHooks: a signal is interpreted before officials arrive
crowds block the approach
the beacon reveals which district reacts first

seatOfPower.name: Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Civic authority made visible through courts, archives, command platforms, and public legitimacy.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. It is not a faction HQ; it is the city's central civic authority. Built for Tormund Vale's giants population, it uses Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas across Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin; water context: River; magic expression: Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone
seatOfPower.civicRole: District 13 authority center for petitions, archives, judgments, and city-scale decisions; not a faction headquarters.
storyHooks: a sealed record contradicts public belief
a petition exposes citywide risk
command platforms become crowd-facing stages

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 / Canal / Riverworks must preserve Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, and Waterwheel Court Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S2 / Granary / Food Market must preserve Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, and Mill Court Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S3 / Civic Services must preserve Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, and Notice Plaza Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S4 / Medical / Healing must preserve Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, and Apothecary Court Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S5 / Arena / Training must preserve Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, and Champion Gate Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Tormund Vale as a giants city using Riverstone, Mudbrick, Timber, Limewash, Canvas across Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin.
Keep Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud as the skyline-scale Wonder, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry as central D13 authority, and Lock House Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Use section landmark distribution to avoid flattening the city: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training.
Water and terrain must follow source context: River; Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin.
requiredVisualElements: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud
elementsToAvoid: oversized human town without river logic
invented district-specific landmarks not supported by sidecars
replacing proper anchor names with generic summaries
collapsing five section spines into one repeated street type
